steps.explanation: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of all three terms: $7x^{6}$, $-2x^{5}$, and $9x^{4}$. The GCF of the coefficients 7, 2, and 9 is 1. The GCF of $x^{6}$, $x^{5}$, and $x^{4}$ is $x^{4}$. So GCF $= x^{4}$.

Evaluating all choices:
  • Choice A: $x^{5}(7x - 2)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5}$, missing the $9x^{4}$ term. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $x^{4}(7x^{2} - 2x + 9)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5} + 9x^{4}$ ✓. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: $x^{4}(7x^{2} - 2x + 1)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5} + x^{4}$, not $9x^{4}$. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: $x^{6}(-2x^{5} + 9x^{4} + 1)$. Expanding gives terms with $x^{11}$ and $x^{10}$, completely different. Incorrect.
